name: Slipped on ice at my Boise job site, is this covered by workers comp or do I sue my boss?
description: Yes, covered by Idaho workers' comp only—no suing employer; report ASAP and file within 1 year.

mainEntity.name: Slipped on ice at my Boise job site, is this covered by workers comp or do I sue my boss?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, covered by Idaho workers' comp only—no suing employer; report ASAP and file within 1 year.
acceptedAnswer.description: In Idaho, workplace slip and falls are typically handled exclusively through workers' compensation, barring lawsuits against your employer unless gross negligence is proven. This no-fault system covers medical costs, wage loss up to 72% of average weekly wage, and disability benefits[2]. Key factors: Must have active Idaho workers' comp insurance; report within 60 days and file claim within 1 year; no personal injury suit possible. Notify employer immediately, seek treatment, gather scene photos. Idaho Industrial Commission oversees claims; hire an attorney if denied.
